questions.question: Which nerve provides the primary sensory innervation to the fibrous pericardium?
A surgeon performing a pericardiocentesis inserts the needle in the left subxiphoid region. Which anatomical space is being accessed to drain excess fluid?
A patient undergoes open-heart surgery. The surgeon identifies a passage posterior to the ascending aorta and pulmonary trunk. Which pericardial structure is being observed?
questions.explanation: Correct answer: B. Transverse pericardial sinus

questions.question: A medical student is asked to name the blood supply to the fibrous pericardium. Which of the following is the primary source?
A patient undergoes heart surgery, and the surgeon needs to clamp the great vessels. Which pericardial sinus is used to place the clamp?
questions.explanation: Correct answer: B. Transverse pericardial sinus

questions.question: During a cardiac procedure, a medical student is asked about the boundaries of the oblique pericardial sinus. Which of the following structures forms its lateral boundary?
A 56-year-old male patient undergoes open-heart surgery. During the procedure, the surgeon introduces a hand behind the left atrium and notices a blind-ended space. What is the name of this space?
questions.explanation: Correct answer: B. Oblique pericardial sinus

questions.question: During cardiac surgery, the surgeon identifies the coronary sulcus. Which of the following structures is located within this sulcus?
questions.explanation: Correct answer: A. Right coronary artery

questions.question: A radiologist is reviewing a CT scan of a patient's heart. The left atrium appears significantly enlarged. Which of the following structures is most likely to occur?
A 50-year-old male with a history of hypertension is found to have left ventricular hypertrophy. Which surface of the heart is mainly formed by the left ventricle?

A 60-year-old man with a history of hypertension presents with crushing retrosternal chest pain radiating to the left shoulder. An ECG confirms an inferior wall myocardial infarction. Which surface of the heart is primarily affected in this condition?
A 45-year-old woman presents with difficulty swallowing. A barium swallow study reveals an external compression on the esophagus. Which cardiac chamber is most likely responsible for this finding?
A stab wound at the left 5th intercostal space near the midclavicular line is likely to injure which part of the heart?
A 65-year-old man with a history of coronary artery disease undergoes an echocardiogram that reveals an enlarged right atrium. Which anatomical border of the heart is most likely to be affected?
A trauma patient is brought to the ER with a penetrating injury to the right second intercostal space at the parasternal line. Which cardiac chamber is most likely injured?

A 58-year-old man presents with exertional dyspnea and bilateral leg swelling. Echocardiography reveals the right ventricular hypertrophy. Which of the following heart surfaces is most likely to show increased thickness?
A 72-year-old male with long-standing hypertension is found to have left ventricular hypertrophy on echocardiography. Which heart borders will likely appear more prominent on a chest X-ray?
A 73-year-old male with long-standing hypertension is found to have right ventricular hypertrophy on echocardiography. Which heart borders will likely appear more prominent on a chest X-ray?
A 60-year-old patient presents with a sinus node dysfunction. Which artery is most likely responsible for supplying the sinoatrial (SA) node in 60% of individuals?
questions.explanation: Correct answer: B. Right coronary artery

questions.question: A patient is undergoing cardiac surgery, and the surgeon identifies the main vein draining blood from the heart into the right atrium. What is the largest vein of the heart?
A stab wound to the anterior interventricular groove could injure which of the following structures?
options: Right coronary artery and small cardiac vein
Anterior interventricular artery and great cardiac vein
Posterior interventricular artery and middle cardiac vein
Circumflex artery and left marginal vein
questions.explanation: Correct answer: B. Anterior interventricular artery and great cardiac vein

questions.question: A cardiothoracic surgeon is performing a procedure involving the posterior interventricular groove. Which artery and vein are most commonly found in this groove?
options: Right coronary artery and small cardiac vein
Left coronary artery and great cardiac vein
Posterior interventricular artery and middle cardiac vein
Circumflex artery and left marginal vein
questions.explanation: Correct answer: C. Posterior interventricular artery and middle cardiac vein

questions.question: A patient presents with an inferior myocardial infarction (ischemia of diaphragmatic surface). Which coronary artery is most likely affected?
questions.explanation: Correct answer: B. Right coronary artery

questions.question: A patient undergoing a cardiac procedure has a catheter inserted into the coronary sinus. Where is the coronary sinus located?
options: In the left posterior coronary sulcus
questions.explanation: Correct answer: B. In the left posterior coronary sulcus

questions.question: A patient with an atrioventricular block has reduced blood flow to the AV node. Which artery supplies the AV node in 80% of individuals?
questions.explanation: Correct answer: A. Posterior interventricular artery

questions.question: Which artery does the great cardiac vein accompany in its course?
questions.explanation: Correct answer: C. Anterior interventricular artery

questions.question: Where is the best location to detect the tricuspid valve?
options: Left 4th intercostal space behind the center of the sternum
Left 5th intercostal space in the midclavicular line
questions.explanation: Correct answer: C. Left 4th intercostal space behind the center of the sternum

questions.question: The right coronary artery gives off a large branch that runs along the lower border of the heart. What is this artery called?
A patient's venogram shows a small vein running along the right posterior coronary sulcus and draining into the coronary sinus. This is most likely which vein?
questions.explanation: Correct answer: C. Small cardiac vein

questions.question: A patient with coronary artery disease has an occlusion in an artery that supplies the pulmonary conus. Which artery is affected?
A patient with an ischemic event is found to have an obstruction of the coronary sinus. Which structure is directly affected by this blockage?
A 50-year-old male presents with severe chest pain. ECG findings suggest a blockage in the artery supplying the left ventricular apex. Which artery is most likely occluded?
questions.explanation: Correct answer: C. Anterior interventricular artery

questions.question: During bypass surgery, the surgeon needs to identify the circumflex artery. It is most commonly found in which location?
questions.explanation: Correct answer: C. Left atrioventricular groove

questions.question: A medical student is asked to locate the aortic valve. At which surface landmark is the valve?
questions.explanation: Correct answer: A. Left 3rd intercostal space

questions.question: A 72-year-old patient presents with ischemia of the apex. Which artery is most likely responsible for its blood supply?
A medical intern is asked about the venous drainage of the myocardium. Which vein primarily drains into the coronary sinus?
questions.explanation: Correct answer: B. Middle cardiac vein

questions.question: Which of the following orifices belong to the right atrium?
The apex of the heart is composed of which of the following heart chambers?

With a myocardial infarction. During angiography, a lesion is found in the artery that supplies the anterior part of the interventricular septum. Which artery is primarily responsible for this region's blood supply?
questions.explanation: Correct answer: D. Left coronary artery

questions.question: Which blood vessel is indicated by the number 5?
options: Circumflex branch of the left coronary artery
questions.explanation: Correct answer: D. Anterior interventricular artery

questions.question: Common venous pool of the body refers to which of the following chamber?
Which of the following orifices belong to the right ventricle?

A 45-year-old male smoker is diagnosed with a myocardial infarction. During angiography, a lesion is found in the artery that supplies the posterior part of the interventricular septum. Which artery is primarily responsible for this region's blood supply?
Which of the following chambers forms the apex of the heart?

A 78-year-old veteran suffers a heart attack and is found in his home unconscious. He is in ventricular tachycardia and is shocked into normal sinus rhythm. He undergoes emergent catheterization and is found to have a thrombosis in the coronary sinus. Which of the following cardiac veins might remain normal?
questions.explanation: Correct answer: B. Anterior cardiac vein

questions.question: A 4-year-old child came to a pediatric heart surgeon to repair a cardiac malformation. A lobulated gland-like structure is seen immediately anterior to the heart and pericardium. Which of the following structures will be found?
Which of the following orifices belong to the left ventricle?

A 44-year-old male with pericardial effusion comes to a local hospital for aspiration of pericardial fluid by pericardiocentesis. The needle is inserted into the pericardial cavity through which of the following intercostal spaces adjacent to the sternum?
questions.explanation: Correct answer: B. Left third intercostal space.
